title: Cheiranthus radicatus
intro: Cheiranthus radicatus, commonly known as Horned Poppy or Hornpoppy, is a perennial plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive horn-like seed pods. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from coughs and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and mucolytic effects. Cheiranthus radicatus is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into cough syrups and respiratory remedies.
taxonomy: Cheiranthus radicatus belongs to the Brassicaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Brassicoideae and Thlaspioidae. The genus Cheiranthus, to which Cheiranthus radicatus belongs, is a small group of annual or perennial flowering plants native to Europe and Asia. The species is a member of the Cheiranthus genus, which is closely related to other genera such as Erysimum and Matthiola. Cheiranthus radicatus is often classified as a subgenus or species of the genus, depending on the source.
